description: Morphospace coordinates for a musical system. 30 dimensions: 18 continuous + 12 categorical. A point in this space defines a generative grammar, not a sound. Real-world traditions are encoded as points; expeditions sample empty regions of the space under a coherence constraint.

continuous._note: All continuous dimensions are normalized to [0.0, 1.0]. Distance metric on continuous dimensions is Euclidean after per-dimension weighting. Sampling and UMAP operate primarily on this subspace.

pitch_density.description: Number of distinct pitches per octave-equivalent cycle, normalized against a 3–60 range. 0.0 = 3-pitch system, 1.0 = 60+ pitch system. Non-octave-repeating systems use pitches-per-primary-cycle.

microtonality_degree.description: Degree of deviation from 12-tone equal temperament. 0.0 = strictly 12-TET, 1.0 = continuous pitch space / freely microtonal with no fixed intervals.

octave_equivalence.description: Strength of octave equivalence as an organizing principle. 1.0 = strict (scales repeat at 2:1), 0.0 = no octave concept (Bohlen-Pierce-like, spectral, or ratio-based without 2:1).

interval_hierarchy.description: Degree to which certain pitches dominate the system. 0.0 = all pitches functionally equal (serialism, some gamelan), 1.0 = strong tonic + clear hierarchical ranking (tonal music, raga with vadi/samvadi).

pitch_flexibility.description: How fixed or inflected pitches are in performance. 0.0 = fixed (piano, fretted instruments), 1.0 = continuously bent/inflected (sitar meends, blues bends, vocal portamento as rule).

metric_periodicity.description: Strictness of metric pulse. 0.0 = non-metric/free (alap, gagaku floating, ambient), 1.0 = strict isochronous meter maintained throughout.

rhythmic_density.description: Events per unit time relative to tempo. 0.0 = extremely sparse (one event per several seconds), 1.0 = near-continuous event stream.

polyrhythmic_complexity.description: Number and independence of simultaneous rhythmic layers. 0.0 = single rhythmic stream, 1.0 = 5+ independent rhythmic strata with irrational ratios.

cycle_length.description: Length of the primary rhythmic cycle, normalized logarithmically. 0.0 = 2-beat cycle, 1.0 = 100+ beat cycle. Non-cyclical systems are 0.0.

tempo_stability.description: Degree to which tempo is held constant. 0.0 = fully rubato / tempo is an expressive parameter, 1.0 = click-track-strict.

piece_duration.description: Target duration of a single performance unit, log-normalized. 0.0 = 30 seconds, 1.0 = 6 hours.

formal_repetition.description: Degree of cyclical repetition vs. through-composition. 0.0 = strictly through-composed, 1.0 = pure cyclical return with no linear development.

development_arc.description: Degree to which the piece exhibits a climactic or developmental arc. 0.0 = static/contemplative (no arc), 1.0 = strong build-climax-release structure.

voice_count.description: Typical number of simultaneous voices/parts, log-normalized. 0.0 = solo, 1.0 = 40+ independent voices.

voice_independence.description: Independence of voices when multiple are present. 0.0 = strict unison/octaves, 1.0 = fully independent polyphony with no shared material.

drone_presence.description: Strength and continuity of drone element. 0.0 = no drone, 1.0 = continuous foundational drone throughout.

improvisation_degree.description: Proportion of musical content generated in performance vs. pre-composed. 0.0 = fully notated/fixed, 1.0 = fully improvised with no pre-existing material.

ornament_density.description: Proportion of performed material that is ornamental (grace notes, meends, gamaks, trills, microtonal inflections) vs. structural notes. 0.0 = no ornamentation, 1.0 = ornament is the primary content.

categorical._note: Categorical dimensions use one-hot or embedded-categorical distance. Coherence checks operate primarily on these (certain combinations are internally contradictory — e.g. 'non_octave_repeating' + strict tonal 'interval_hierarchy' near 1.0).

tuning_basis.description: The mathematical foundation generating the pitch set.

scale_topology.description: How the pitch set repeats (or doesn't) across registers.

formal_type.description: How a piece unfolds at the macro level.

ornament_grammar.description: The role and status of ornamentation.

transmission_mode.description: How the tradition is taught and preserved.

social_function.description: Why the music exists; what it's for.

listener_role.description: The expected role of whoever hears the music.

silence_treatment.description: The grammatical status of silence within the system.

temporal_framing.description: What determines when a piece begins and ends.

coherence_constraints._note: Constraints the coherence checker enforces before committing a sampled point. Violating these produces an internally contradictory grammar.

hard_contradictions.reason: Cannot claim octave equivalence in a non-octave-repeating pitch system.
Pulse-free systems cannot have strong metric periodicity.
Tempo stability requires a pulse to be stable against.
Monophonic texture implies a single voice.
Voice independence is undefined for single-voice textures.
Drone-based textures must have a present drone.
Forbidden ornamentation cannot coexist with high ornament density.
Fully improvised systems cannot be prescriptively notated.

soft_warnings.note: Very long pieces with strict meter are rare but not impossible (minimalism, some trance traditions).
Large-ensemble improvisation is rare but occurs (New Orleans, some free jazz, some African traditions).
Ensemble microtonal coordination is difficult; real examples exist (Arabic takht, Turkish classical) but are rare in space.

distance_metric.note: Per-dimension weights can be tuned. Expeditions prioritize high distance from all known corpus points, subject to coherence_constraints passing.
